subject: [CCSDK] Fix error logs due to empty URL
commit_message:
[CCSDK] Fix error logs due to empty URL

The gating tests for Honolulu have reported flooding of error messages
in SDNC because the ricLink in values.yaml was not set.
The information about RICs can now be provided in values.yaml

Change-Id: I8ef67c27ed95432fb90de88b2976f39068fa7be0
Issue-ID: CCSDK-3227
Signed-off-by: RehanRaza <muhammad.rehan.raza@est.tech>
[Improve ric list generation]
Signed-off-by: Krzysztof Opasiak <k.opasiak@samsung.com>

Patch Set 3:

This suggested enhanced change would be quite substantial, and unfair to ask for it to be merged for Honolulu. 

(This entire way of configuring RICs via OOM needs a fundamental rethink anyway) 

So I suggest abandoning this change for now - to be fixed in Istanbul.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(3 comments)

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> This suggested enhanced change would be quite substantial, and unfair to ask for it to be merged for Honolulu. 
> 

Well I considered it as a fix to our helm charts so I've been happy to take it even after RC0.

> (This entire way of configuring RICs via OOM needs a fundamental rethink anyway) 
> 
> So I suggest abandoning this change for now - to be fixed in Istanbul.

My understanding was that currently if ricLink is empty then SDNC gets somehow broken? Isn't it the case?

If it is then I can take the patch as is as a quick fix or if you have issues with helm I can help you with the suggested solution

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Patch Set 3:
> > 
> > This suggested enhanced change would be quite substantial, and unfair to ask for it to be merged for Honolulu. 
> > 
> 
> Well I considered it as a fix to our helm charts so I've been happy to take it even after RC0.
> 
> > (This entire way of configuring RICs via OOM needs a fundamental rethink anyway) 
> > 
> > So I suggest abandoning this change for now - to be fixed in Istanbul.
> 
> My understanding was that currently if ricLink is empty then SDNC gets somehow broken? Isn't it the case?
> 
> If it is then I can take the patch as is as a quick fix or if you have issues with helm I can help you with the suggested solution

That's exactly right - without this fix, the RIC ip ends up mangled, and the a1 policy manager keeps polling SDNC with a missing RIC ... and SDNC keeps logging that message over and over and over, to the point where the logs roll and we miss real log messages.  This is why it took about 2 weeks to diagnose the issue in our version bump change.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1
> 
> > Patch Set 3:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 3:
> > > 
> > > This suggested enhanced change would be quite substantial, and unfair to ask for it to be 
> > > merged for Honolulu. 
> > > 
> > 
> > Well I considered it as a fix to our helm charts so I've been happy to take it even after RC0.
> > 
> > > (This entire way of configuring RICs via OOM needs a fundamental rethink anyway) 
> > > 
> > > So I suggest abandoning this change for now - to be fixed in Istanbul.
> > 
> > My understanding was that currently if ricLink is empty then SDNC gets somehow broken? Isn't it
> > the case?
> > 
> > If it is then I can take the patch as is as a quick fix or if you have issues with helm I can help 
> > you with the suggested solution
> 
> That's exactly right - without this fix, the RIC ip ends up mangled, and the a1 policy manager 
> keeps polling SDNC with a missing RIC ... and SDNC keeps logging that message over and over and
> over, to the point where the logs roll and we miss real log messages.  This is why it took about
> 2 weeks to diagnose the issue in our version bump change.

A fix has been submitted in CCSDK to check for such a mis-configuration 
When a mis-configuration is found the RIC configuration is rejected - With a single ERROR entry in the log, then there will be no further log entry. 
Since the RIC configuration is rejected there is not periodic attempt to sync - so no repeated log error entry. 
The log flooding is then prevented. 

(Of course "normal" operation will still create "normal" INFO log entries - and this is probably too verbose - but we will address that in Istanbul 

Thanks for offering to merge a fix to the mis-configuration mentioned - even after RC0 - so we will rush to get this fix available ASAP.
Hashtag added: bugfix
Hashtag added: honoluluCandidate

> When a mis-configuration is found the RIC configuration is rejected - With a single ERROR entry in the log, then there will be no further log entry. 
> Since the RIC configuration is rejected there is not periodic attempt to sync - so no repeated log error entry. 
> The log flooding is then prevented. 
> 
> (Of course "normal" operation will still create "normal" INFO log entries - and this is probably too verbose - but we will address that in Istanbul 
> 
> Thanks for offering to merge a fix to the mis-configuration mentioned - even after RC0 - so we will rush to get this fix available ASAP.

Ok guys so how do you want to proceed:

1) We abandon this change and wait for code fix

2) We marge it as is (then I'd prefer to just surround it with if which is a little bit cleaner)

3) You want me to do the 2 loops that would allow you go generate multiple entries here

I can help you either with 2 or 3 but I need to know your intentions;)

Patch Set 3:

We're now testing a new change locally to correctly create the configuration. 
(Its a little tricky to generate the JSON syntactically correct from the template - and tricky to test)
We will commit for review as soon as possible - a few hours max hopefully. 


We have also added the fix to CCSDK to check for blank RIC URLs too. 
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/ccsdk/oran/+/119896
